claim: PydanticAI simplifies model swapping; change from GPT-4o to Gemini or smaller models with single parameter change; Anthropic support coming
actionable: Use PydanticAI framework for model-agnostic agent development to avoid lock-in; easily swap providers by changing model parameter

claim: Agent decides autonomously between 3-5 search queries based on question complexity; request for simple facts triggers fewer searches than detailed analysis
actionable: Design agent systems to adaptively determine search budget; allow models to decide query count based on task complexity for token efficiency

claim: Tavily API offers 1000 free monthly calls; Duck Duck Go free but rate-limits searches; choose based on throughput needs
actionable: For agent search tools, use Tavily for production (1K free calls/month) or Duck Duck Go with proxy for high-volume searches

claim: Injecting current date into system prompt enables agents to search for recent news; agents recognize date context and use it to guide queries
actionable: When building research agents, inject current date into system prompt to enable time-aware search behavior; agents will use it to filter results

claim: Structured output with Pydantic dataclass enforces output schema; agent must include research title, main body, and bullet points in specific format
actionable: Use Pydantic dataclasses to define required output structure for agents; ensures consistent, parseable responses without additional parsing

claim: Research agent with 4 parallel async searches and 3 results each consumed 3700 tokens; token economy matters for research agents
actionable: When building research agents, budget for multiple search requests; monitor token usage and optimize search result reduction if cost-sensitive

claim: Dependencies pattern allows runtime injection of max_results and system prompt updates; enables UI-driven configuration without code changes
actionable: Use dependency injection in agents to enable runtime configuration of search parameters and system prompts via UI or API

claim: Async search tools execute in parallel; all 4 search queries run concurrently reducing total latency compared to sequential searches
actionable: Design agent tool interfaces to support async execution; leverage parallelism to reduce agent response latency on independent tasks
